text.title: AI Feedback Triage Protocol Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns from real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Hallucination tickets binned as generic 'bug' lose ML signal. Postmortem can't distinguish prompt regression from data drift.
antipattern.root-cause: Generic feedback taxonomy with no AI lanes.
antipattern.fix: Add hallucination / refusal / wrong-tool / out-of-scope / cost-spike as first-class lanes; route to ML eng.

antipattern.symptom: Triage protocol no one owns. After 90 days half the lanes are stale, new failure classes uncovered.
antipattern.root-cause: Named owner missing at protocol top.
antipattern.fix: Assign a single accountable owner. Stale protocol = owner's retro topic.

antipattern.symptom: Branch reads 'if AI-related → lane ML'. Operators interpret differently; reconciliation needed weekly.
antipattern.root-cause: Decision branch signal is prose, not observable.
antipattern.fix: Rewrite branch as 'if ticket.labels contains hallucination OR feedback.score < 0.5 then lane=ML' — observable, automatable.

antipattern.symptom: AI incidents triaged but never converted to golden-set candidates. Same bug class returns quarterly.
antipattern.root-cause: No bridge between triage protocol and eval-pipeline curation.
antipattern.fix: Add step 'incident → golden candidate within 7 days of close'; tie to golden-set-curation methodology.
